title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Hometown Stories
subTitle: Intimate portraits of people from around Japan, each leading diverse lives while enriching lives of others, with themes ranging from ancient traditions to contemporary social issues.

title: Sumopedia
subTitle: A Sumo wrestling referee's attires hold meaning behind the colourful traditional look and is actually believed that it symbolises the victory.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Journeys in Japan
subTitle: Dean Newcombe explores Kushimoto, the southernmost town on Japan's main island, which is home to geological wonders, rich marine habitats-including sea turtles, warm people, and riveting festivals.

subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Dining with the Chef

title: Medical Frontiers
subTitle: Explores the impact of GLP-1 receptor agonists on obesity management, while highlighting the significant risks associated with unsupervised use and the wider therapeutic potential of these treatments.

title: Kabuki Kool
subTitle: The historic figure of Minamoto no Yoshitsune has been immortalised in many kabuki plays as a child, a skilled warrior, and a tragic hero. Explore with actor Kataoka Ainosuke.

title: Direct Talk

subTitle: Cyrus Nozomu Sethna discovers the stories behind frozen food vending machines and talks to experts in the industry.

subTitle: Comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia. It explains the day's developments and what is behind them.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Since 2021, Yoshida Hirofumi has been principal guest conductor of the Odesa National Academic Opera and Ballet Theater in Ukraine. He talks about what he hopes to convey through music amid conflict.

title: Herbal Symphony
subTitle: Japan's oldest private herb garden in Uda, Nara Prefecture has a 300-year history. To pass this legacy into the future, knowledge of medicinal herbs is shared with younger generations.

subTitle: A look at the rich and fascinating Edo era of Japan, which was dominated by a deep love for the arts. The traditional woodblock prints called Ukiyo-e blossomed during this period of time.

subTitle: Highlighting professionals at the forefront of different fields by showcasing their amazing work, discovering their motivations, and learning more about the challenges they may face in the future.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: Japanese strawberries are large and sweet and are mainly eaten fresh. Visiting Tochigi, Japan's largest production site, to meet farmers, check out a unique strawberry research centre and more.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Sekiguchi Itaru, who worked on restoring the pipe organ at Notre Dame after it was destroyed in the 2019 fire, discusses his work, including the tuning and voicing of musical instruments.

title: The Seasons of Yamato
subTitle: A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with the pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces. This time, Shunbun.

title: Hit the Road
subTitle: A drive through Japan's Hiroshima Prefecture on a trip to reach the famed Itsukushima Shrine. Along the way, travels off the beaten path to make stops at some exceptional locations.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: Centuries ago, Tokyo was known as EDO. More than a million people enjoyed life in this city, they live on in ukiyo-e woodblock prints. Taking a deep dive into ukiyo-e and exploring the soul of old Tokyo.
